title: Review: wolftpm/wolftpm-sys/wolftpm-tss crates
notes:
Maintainer risk: LOW — No correctness bugs found. Core logic (FMT1 modifier stripping, transient handle cleanup, setenv mutex) is sound. Two P1 maintainability issues: missing Send on TSS types, and a stale NOTE that will mislead future maintainers.
Confidence: 10% chance of missed bug. Least confident flags: (1) TssError::get() return type and sign — if it returns i32, the 'as u32' cast in wolftpm-tss/src/error.rs:131 could produce unexpected values for negative codes; (2) wolfTPM thread-local state in future versions — the Send safety argument is documented for v4.0.0 only. Uncertainty driven by: cannot fully audit the tpm-rs library's TssError type without fetching the repo; cannot verify wolfTPM's absence of thread-local state at runtime without a hardware TPM.
Idiom rating: CLEAN — No ownership antipatterns, no gratuitous clone, no unwrap in production paths, SAFETY comments on all unsafe, proper error enum with non_exhaustive. Two minor issues: missing Hash derive on TpmRc, and a redundant as-u32 cast on TssError::get().
Rust idiom confidence: 85% — no missed issues likely. Least confident flags: (1) TssError::get() return type verified against git source; (2) Checked for all common clippy patterns. Uncertainty driven by: cannot run clippy directly in this session to catch any remaining automated warnings.
